steps.title: Fit vertex form in Desmos
steps.content: The maximum depth is the vertex $(6,302.4)$, so use $g=a(t-6)^2+302.4$. The seal reaches the surface at $t=12$, so another point is $(12,0)$. Enter those points and fit the coefficient with $d_1\sim a(t_1-6)^2+302.4$.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: Desmos returns $a=-8.4$, so the depth model is $g(t)=-8.4(t-6)^2+302.4$. This is the same result as substituting $(12,0)$ into vertex form.

steps.title: Evaluate at t = 10 minutes
steps.content: Evaluate the model at $t=10$: $g(10)=-8.4(10-6)^2+302.4=-8.4(16)+302.4=168$. The estimated depth was 168 meters.
